name: Tinnitus Handicap Inventory
description: A self-report measure to quantify the impact of tinnitus on daily living
instructions: The purpose of this questionnaire is to identify difficulties that you may be experiencing because of your tinnitus. Please answer every question. For each question, select the response that best applies to you.

questions.label: Because of your tinnitus, is it difficult for you to concentrate?
Does the loudness of your tinnitus make it difficult for you to hear people?
Does your tinnitus make you angry?
Does your tinnitus make you feel confused?
Because of your tinnitus, do you feel desperate?

subscales.name: Functional
subscales.description: Mental, social, occupational, and physical functioning limitations

subscales.name: Catastrophic
subscales.description: Desperate reactions and perceived inability to cope

severityBands.label: Mild Handicap
severityBands.description: Easily masked by environmental sounds; easily forgotten with activities

severityBands.label: Moderate Handicap
severityBands.description: May be noticed even in presence of background noise; still can perform daily activities

severityBands.label: Severe Handicap
severityBands.description: Almost always heard; leads to disturbance of sleep patterns and can interfere with daily activities

severityBands.label: Catastrophic Handicap
severityBands.description: Always heard; leads to difficulty with any activity

riskRules.message: Patient reports feeling desperate due to tinnitus - requires clinical attention

interpretation.clinicalNotes: The THI is a psychometrically robust and easy-to-administer measure that can be used in clinical and research settings.
interpretation.note: This is a skeleton with 5 sample questions. The full THI has 25 questions.
